forecasts.reasoning: The question requires "tariff" or "tariffs" to be absent from BOTH the NYT Today's Paper page and the WSJ print edition front page on a SINGLE day out of a 42-day window (May 19 to June 30). Even if Trump's trade policies make "tariff" a highly frequent word, achieving at least one day without its appearance in the headline/summary blurbs of NYT and the front page of WSJ is highly probable over a period encompassing 6 weeks. Weekends in particular (Saturdays/Sundays) and holidays like Memorial Day significantly reduce hard news and business reporting, lowering the daily probability of 'tariff' appearing. Furthermore, WSJ does not print a Sunday edition, meaning on Sundays the WSJ condition might be trivially satisfied or drastically reduced depending on how the URL resolves, placing the burden solely on NYT. Over 42 independent or semi-independent news days, the cumulative probability of at least one day without the word is very high.
